title: Retrofuture Command Center
summary: A dense AI-native interface grammar with editorial hierarchy, visible context chips, trace surfaces, and restrained cyber instrumentation.

retrieval.description: Use when the requested product needs an AI-native workspace, control room, builder shell, dense expert UI, or cyber/editorial visual identity.

donorSources.label: Material 3 Expressive
donorSources.notes: Expressive tokens, motion, and emotional hierarchy as a system, not decoration.

donorSources.label: Apple Human Interface Guidelines
donorSources.notes: Dynamic materials and focus rules should clarify hierarchy.

donorSources.label: Figma canvas for agents
donorSources.notes: AI work happens inside the canvas with visible context and design-system awareness.

donorSources.label: Vercel AI Elements
donorSources.notes: Message parts, tool calls, reasoning displays, and streaming states as UI primitives.

donorSources.label: WCAG 2.2
donorSources.notes: Focus visibility, target size, dragging alternatives, and accessibility gates.

prompt: Design the surface as a working command center, not a decorative dark dashboard.
Use a deliberate display/body/mono typography triad, tabular numerals for telemetry, and one dominant accent color.
Every AI surface must show context chips, streaming or trace state, and a clear continue/commit action.
Use retro/cyber elements only as information instrumentation: labels, scanlines, trace rails, status chips, and structured logs.
requiredElements: Clear primary action in the main panel
Context chips or visible inputs behind any AI output
Trace or evidence drawer for agent/tool activity
Keyboard-visible focus states and non-color status indicators
Mobile fallback layout that preserves action order
forbiddenPatterns: system font stack as the only typographic choice
allowedSubstitutions: If advanced motion is unavailable, preserve hierarchy with opacity, border, and tokenized spacing.
If canvas interactions are unavailable, provide a structured list fallback with the same actions.
If a display font cannot be installed, choose an existing non-system brand font already in the project.
stackHints: React/Vite surfaces can use CSS variables, motion, and component-level state.
Server-rendered surfaces should preserve the shell, trace, and command rail with progressive enhancement.
Respect prefers-reduced-motion and provide static equivalents for large transitions.

validators.description: Fail if the surface can be summarized as a generic dark/glass/card dashboard without a unique product signature.
validators.evidence: DOM text, screenshots, CSS class names, and visual verdict notes.

validators.description: AI surfaces must expose context, trace/evidence, and a continue or commit path.
validators.evidence: Rendered controls, accessible labels, and trace panel screenshot.

validators.description: Motion must not be required to understand state or complete the flow.

provenance.notes: Seeded from parallel 2026 design-trend research for Retrobuilder.
